body: Suresh ji, aapke Weekday Lunch Thali @ ₹149 offer ko dekhkar corporate bulk thali package ke liye kuch ideas hai. 4200 unique customers humare paas hai, jisme 42% repeat customers hai. Aapke thali package ko bulk mein kitne logon ke liye design karna chahiye? Aur kya special services aap add karna chahte hain?
rationale: Used the trigger payload to initiate a conversation about corporate bulk thali packages, leveraging the merchant's active offer and customer data to make the discussion specific and actionable.

body: Padma, aapka kids yoga summer camp idea bahut achha hai! ₹499 pe First Month ki offer ke saath, aapke paas 95 active members hain. Kya aap summer camp ke liye kuch specific dates aur pricing soch rahe hain? Hum aapki planning mein madad kar sakte hain.
rationale: I used the active_planning_intent trigger and the kids_yoga_summer_camp payload to create a message that encourages Padma to share her thoughts on the summer camp, leveraging the current offer and member base to inform her planning.

body: Hi Aditya, reminder: you have an appointment tomorrow at Karim's Salon in Alambagh, Lucknow. Please arrive 10 minutes prior to your scheduled time. Don't forget to let us know if you have any specific hair concerns or preferences, hum aapko behtar service de sakte hain
rationale: Used a reminder with specific time and address hint to help the customer prepare for their appointment, and added a prep note to gather information about their hair concerns or preferences to provide better service.

body: Namaste Riya, aapka kal ka appointment hai Beauty Lounge by Renu, Gomti Nagar mein. Kripya 10 minute pehle aayein, taaki aapka service time par shuru ho sake. Aapko koi specific service leni hai, ya humse koi sawal hai?
rationale: Used loss aversion by reminding the customer of their appointment tomorrow, and added a practical note about arriving 10 minutes early to ensure timely service, leveraging the warm and practical tone suitable for salons.

body: Ramesh ji, garmi ke mausam mein ORS, sunscreen aur antifungal ki demand 40%, 38% aur 45% badh rahi hai. Aapki shelf ko audit karna zaruri hai, taaki aap apne customers ko sahi samay par sahi products de saken. Aap kya karna chahte hain?
rationale: Using the trigger payload to highlight the increasing demand for specific products during the summer season, and prompting the merchant to take action to audit their shelf and prepare for the demand.

body: Dr. Meera, aapke high-risk adult patients ke liye 3-month fluoride varnish recall ka fayda hai, JIDA Oct 2026 ke research ke anusar. Aapne isse apne clinic mein lagu kiya hai? Dental Cleaning @ ₹299 ke saath aapke patients ko yeh service kyun de sakte hain?
rationale: Using the latest digest item to prompt the dentist to reassess recall intervals for high-risk adult patients and offering a relevant service, leveraging the active offer Dental Cleaning @ ₹299.

body: Namaste Mr. Sharma, aapke chronic medicines ki refill due hai. Metformin, atorvastatin, aur telmisartan ki stock 28 April ko khatam ho jayegi. Aapko Free Home Delivery > ₹499 ka fayda milega. Kya aap abhi order dena chahte hain?
rationale: Using loss aversion and convenience, reminding the customer about their refill due date and offering free home delivery to prompt a timely order.

body: Hi Vivaan, aapka chronic medication refill due hai. Aaj hi Bright Smile Dental par visit karke apni refill shuru kara sakte hain, 2 minutes mein. Abhi YES karke appointment book karein.
rationale: Used loss aversion and convenience to prompt the customer to refill their medication, and offered a quick 2-minute process to book an appointment.

body: Naye dental clinic ke saath comparison karna chahte hain? Aapki Dental Cleaning @ ₹299 ki offer kaisi hai, aur kya aap apne patients ko behtar services de sakte hain? YES/STOP
rationale: Used voyeur curiosity to pique the merchant's interest in comparing their services with a new competitor, framing it as a way to see how they compare.

body: Naye restaurant ke saath comparison karna chahte hain? Aapki Weekday Lunch Thali @ ₹149 kitni popular hai? YES/STOP
rationale: Used voyeur curiosity to pique the merchant's interest in comparing their performance with the new competitor, framing it as a way to see how they stack up.

body: Lakshmi ji, aapke salon mein is week kaunsa service sabse zyada demand mein hai? Haircut @ ₹99 ya Hair Spa @ ₹499?
rationale: I used curiosity to ask about the most in-demand service, leveraging the merchant's active offers and high engagement signals to spark a conversation.

body: Suresh ji, aapke cafe mein sabse busy din kaunsa hota hai? Tuesday/Wednesday ke matches ke din orders badhte hain ya weekend par?
rationale: I used curiosity to ask about the busiest day, considering the recent IPL match data and its impact on restaurant orders, to understand Suresh's business better.

body: Hi Rashmi, it's been 57 days since your last visit to PowerHouse Fitness. We noticed you were focused on weight loss during your previous 5 months of membership. What's holding you back from achieving your fitness goals now?
rationale: Used the customer's previous focus and membership history to personalize the message and encourage them to share their current challenges, leveraging the trigger payload for context.

body: Hi Reyansh, aapko 30 din ho gaye Asha Dental Care mein aaye hue. Aaj hi Dental Cleaning @ ₹299 book kijiye, aapki dental health ke liye yeh zaroori hai. YES/STOP?
rationale: Used loss aversion and social proof by mentioning the time lapsed since the last visit and offering a specific service to re-engage the customer.

body: Reyansh, aap 1 month se humse medicines nahi lekar aaye. Aapki health humare liye bahut important hai. Hum aapko Free BP & Sugar Check ka offer dena chahte hain. Kya aap is offer ka labh uthana chahte hain? YES/STOP
rationale: Used social proof and loss aversion by mentioning the customer's name and the time lapsed since their last visit, and offered a specific health check service to re-engage them.

body: Anjali, kya aapke salon mein is week koi naya offer ya service launch ho raha hai? Hum aapki business ke baare mein jaanne ke liye utsuk hain!
rationale: I used curiosity to re-engage the merchant by asking an open-ended question about their business, leveraging the Hindi-English code-mix to match their language preference.

body: Anand, aapke cafe mein is week kaun sa dish sabse zyada popular ho raha hai? Kya koi naya combo try kiya hai?
rationale: Used curiosity to re-engage the merchant by asking about their popular dish this week, leveraging the Hindi-English code-mix to match their language preference.

body: Diwali aa rahi hai, sirf 188 din baaki hain! Maine aapke liye ek campaign taiyar kiya hai - Haircut @ ₹99 + FREE head massage. Aapke Studio11 Family Salon ke liye yeh offer bahut achha hoga. Main post taiyar kar dunga, aapke permission ke saath. Kya main post karoon? YES/STOP
rationale: I used effort externalization by drafting a ready-to-post campaign and offering to handle the posting process, making it easy for the merchant to participate in the Diwali promotion.

body: Holi ke liye sirf 10 din bache hain! Mainne ek campaign taiyar kiya hai - 'Holi Fitness Challenge' - jisme aapke gym ke liye naye trial walk-ins laaye jaa sakte hain. Isme 3 FREE Trial Classes ka offer bhi shamil hai. Main isse post karne ke liye taiyar hoon, aapke permission ke saath. Kya main isse post kar sakti hoon? YES/STOP
rationale: I used the festival_upcoming trigger and created a ready-to-post campaign to leverage the Holi festival, providing a clear call-to-action for the merchant to approve the post.

body: Vikas ji, aapka BP check machine verify nahi hai. Aapke paas postcard ya phone call ke through verification karne ka option hai. Verified BP check machine se aapke customers ko trustworthy service milega aur aapke business mein 30% tak uplift ho sakta hai. Aap kya karna chahte hain?
rationale: I used the trigger payload to create a specific and actionable message, highlighting the benefits of verifying the BP check machine and the potential uplift in business, and asked an open-ended question to encourage the merchant to respond.

body: Aaj DC vs MI ka match hai, Arun Jaitley Stadium mein! SK Pizza Junction pe aapke liye kya special offer hai? Aapke customers ko kya attract karne ke liye aap kuch plan kar rahe hain?
rationale: Used the IPL match trigger to prompt the merchant to think about special offers for the match day, leveraging the local event to attract customers, and considering the merchant's current trial subscription and performance metrics.

body: Suresh ji, aapke Mylari South Indian Cafe ke 145 reviews ho gaye hain! Ab aap Indiranagar ke top 10% South Indian restaurants mein shamil ho gaye hain. Aapki kya plans hain aage badhne ke liye?
rationale: Used social proof to highlight the merchant's achievement and asked a low-friction follow-on question to encourage engagement.

body: Mukesh, aapka Pizza Spot ab Aundh, Pune ke top 10% restaurants mein shaamil ho gaya hai! Aapke 889 unique customers ne aapki restaurant ko chuna hai. Ab aage kya plan hai? Koi naya offer launch karne ka plan hai?
rationale: Used social proof to highlight the merchant's achievement and asked a low-friction follow-on question to encourage engagement and potentially unlock new offers.

body: Bharat ji, aapke calls 50% kam ho gaye hain last 7 days mein, jo ki peer benchmark se bhi kam hai. Aapki visibility khone ki samasya hai, kya aap ab action lena chahte hain? YES/STOP
rationale: Using loss aversion to highlight the significant drop in calls, framing it as a loss of visibility to prompt immediate action.

body: Sushma, aapke salon ki visibility thodi kam ho rahi hai. Aapke views 8% kam ho gaye hain, jo ki peer benchmark se zyada hai. Abhi action lena zaroori hai, nahi to aap aur bhi customers ko lose kar sakte hain. Kya aap abhi apni strategy ko badalna chahti hain? YES/STOP
rationale: I used loss aversion by highlighting the drop in views and framing it as a potential loss of customers, and provided a binary CTA to prompt immediate action.

body: Padma ji, aapke Zen Yoga Studio ke calls mein 15% ki spike aayi hai, 18 calls se ab tak! Let's lock in this momentum, aap kya soch rahe hain - kya hume abhi aur kids yoga classes add karne chahiye?
rationale: Celebrating the specific 15% spike in calls with the merchant, then pivoting to lock in momentum with a concrete next action, leveraging the likely driver of kids yoga post to prompt further action.

body: Vikas ji, aapke Sunrise Medicos ke performance mein spike dekha gaya hai - 7 din mein views mein 2% aur calls mein 5% ka increment! Ab let's lock in this momentum. Aap kya sochte hain ki aapke pharmacy ke liye agle kadam kya hona chahiye?
rationale: Celebrated the performance spike with specific numbers and pivoted to lock in momentum, encouraging the merchant to think about the next step with an open-ended question.

body: Priya, aapki last cleaning ke baad 6 mahine ho gaye hain. Aapki dental health maintain karne ke liye hum aapko 2 slots offer kar rahe hain: Wed 5 Nov, 6pm ya Thu 6 Nov, 5pm, dono ke saath Dental Cleaning @ ₹299. Kaunsa slot aapke liye suitable hai?
rationale: Using recall_due trigger to remind Priya of her due dental cleaning, offering two specific slots with a fixed price to encourage her to book an appointment.

body: Hi Diya, it's been 28 days since your last visit to Zen Yoga Studio. We miss you! Book your next session now and choose from: Yoga Class @ ₹499 for the first month or Free Body Composition Analysis. Which one works for you?
rationale: Used recall_due trigger and offered concrete slots with price to re-engage the lapsed customer, leveraging the high retention signal of the gym.

body: Dr. Meera, DCI ne radiograph dose limits revise kiye hain, ab 1.0 mSv tak simit hai. Aapka X-ray setup audit karna zaroori hai, E-speed film ya RVG sensors document karein. Aapki clinic ke liye kya kadam uthane hain?
rationale: Using the trigger payload, I leveraged the regulation change to prompt Dr. Meera to take action on her X-ray setup, making the message specific and actionable, while also using Hindi-English code-mix to match the merchant's language preference.
